title: lastrea filix-mas subvar. nidus
intro: Lastrea filix-mas subvar. nidus, commonly known as the lady fern, is a type of flowering plant in the fern family (Dryopteridaceae) with delicate, lacy fronds and a distinctive subterranean rhizome. Traditionally, the lady fern has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making it effective in treating various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The medicinal properties of Lastrea filix-mas subvar. nidus are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the rhizome of the lady fern is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
